name: ISO 21448: Road vehicles — Safety of the Intended Functionality (SOTIF)
disclaimer: Informative only. Not a substitute for reading the published standard.

entries.title: Specification and design
entries.summary: Covers activities for specifying the intended functionality and identifying potential insufficiencies in the system design.

entries.title: Specification of the intended functionality
entries.summary: Requires a clear description of the system's intended functionality, operational design domain, and expected behavior.

entries.title: Definition of acceptance criteria
entries.summary: Mandates defining quantitative and qualitative acceptance criteria for the absence of unreasonable risk from functional insufficiencies.

entries.title: Identification and evaluation of hazards caused by functional insufficiencies
entries.summary: Describes the systematic identification and evaluation of hazards arising from functional insufficiencies of the intended functionality.

entries.title: General approach to hazard identification
entries.summary: Outlines the overall approach for identifying hazards that can result from system functional insufficiencies or foreseeable misuse.

entries.title: Identification of functional insufficiencies
entries.summary: Requires systematic identification of sensor limitations, algorithm weaknesses, and actuator insufficiencies that may cause hazardous behavior.

entries.title: Identification of triggering conditions
entries.summary: Mandates identification of specific conditions (environmental, operational, human) that can trigger hazardous behavior through functional insufficiencies.

entries.title: Hazardous event classification
entries.summary: Defines how to classify hazardous events resulting from functional insufficiencies based on severity, exposure, and controllability.

entries.title: Identification and evaluation of triggering conditions
entries.summary: Provides detailed methods for identifying and evaluating triggering conditions that may lead to hazardous scenarios.

entries.title: Triggering condition analysis methods
entries.summary: Specifies systematic analysis methods such as scenario analysis and fault injection for discovering triggering conditions.

entries.title: Sensor-related triggering conditions
entries.summary: Addresses triggering conditions related to sensor performance limitations including weather, lighting, occlusion, and interference.

entries.title: Algorithm-related triggering conditions
entries.summary: Covers triggering conditions caused by algorithm limitations such as edge cases, insufficient training, or performance degradation.

entries.title: User-related triggering conditions
entries.summary: Identifies triggering conditions arising from foreseeable user behavior, including misuse and failure to intervene when expected.

entries.title: Functional modification and risk reduction
entries.summary: Describes activities to improve the system design to reduce identified risks from functional insufficiencies and triggering conditions.

entries.title: Risk treatment options
entries.summary: Defines risk treatment strategies including functional improvement, restriction of operational domain, and driver warning mechanisms.

entries.title: Functional improvements to reduce risk
entries.summary: Specifies approaches for reducing risk through design improvements such as sensor fusion, algorithm enhancement, and redundancy.

entries.title: Restriction of operational conditions
entries.summary: Addresses limiting the operational conditions under which the system is active as a means to reduce exposure to triggering conditions.

entries.title: Verification and validation
entries.summary: Covers verification of design measures and validation that the residual risk from functional insufficiencies is acceptably low.

entries.title: Verification of SOTIF-related design measures
entries.summary: Requires verification that design modifications intended to reduce SOTIF risks are correctly implemented and effective.

entries.title: Validation strategy
entries.summary: Mandates a documented validation strategy combining multiple methods to demonstrate acceptable residual risk from functional insufficiencies.

entries.title: Scenario-based validation methods
entries.summary: Specifies the use of scenario catalogs, logical scenarios, and concrete test scenarios for validating SOTIF-related safety.

entries.title: Simulation-based validation
entries.summary: Addresses the use of simulation for validation, including requirements for simulation credibility and environment fidelity.

entries.title: Real-world testing
entries.summary: Covers physical testing on proving grounds and public roads as part of the validation strategy for SOTIF.

entries.title: Statistical validation and acceptance criteria
entries.summary: Defines statistical methods and criteria for determining when sufficient validation evidence has been gathered to accept residual risk.

entries.title: Evaluation of residual risk
entries.summary: Covers the final evaluation of residual risk to determine whether the SOTIF-related risk is acceptable for release.

entries.title: Known and unknown hazardous scenarios
entries.summary: Distinguishes between known hazardous scenarios (identified and mitigated) and unknown hazardous scenarios (residual unknowns), defining the four-area model.

entries.title: Residual risk quantification
entries.summary: Provides approaches for quantifying residual risk including probabilistic methods and exposure-based analyses.

entries.title: Acceptance of residual risk
entries.summary: Defines the criteria and evidence required to determine that residual SOTIF risk is sufficiently low for the intended application.

entries.title: Post-release monitoring
entries.summary: Addresses ongoing activities after product release to maintain SOTIF throughout the product lifetime.

entries.title: Field monitoring for SOTIF
entries.summary: Requires establishment of field monitoring processes to detect previously unknown triggering conditions and functional insufficiencies.

entries.title: Incident analysis and feedback
entries.summary: Mandates systematic analysis of field incidents related to SOTIF and feeding findings back into the development process.

entries.title: Continuous improvement
entries.summary: Addresses the use of field data and new knowledge to continuously improve the system and reduce residual risk over time.
